draft.introduction: The greatest commandment dialogue. A scribe asks which commandment is first, and Jesus answers with the Shema (Deut 6:4) and the love of neighbor (Lev 19:18), uniting the two as inseparable.

translation.text: And one of the scribes asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?” Jesus answered, “The most important is: Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one. And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength. The second is this: You shall love your neighbor as yourself. There is no other commandment greater than these.”
translation.original_text: ἄκουε Ισραηλ κύριος ὁ θεὸς ἡμῶν κύριος εἷς ἐστιν

translator_notes.note: Mark alone among the Synoptics includes the full Shema declaration: “The Lord our God, the Lord is one” (κύριος εἷς ἐστιν). This monotheistic confession is the foundation on which the love commands rest—ethics flows from theology.
